title: Scheduling, Affinity, Taints, Topology, Priority, Preemption, and Eviction · Reconciliation Brief: K8s Taints Tolerations Conditions
summary: A critical workload remains Pending despite free aggregate capacity and later concentrates replicas into one failure domain while lower-priority work is disrupted. Planned artifact: Add and verify the k8s taints tolerations conditions increment in placement and disruption decision record. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and evidence defense.
objectives: Trace NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule, and NoExecute taints, matching tolerations, toleration time, node conditions, and admission-added behavior.
Design inter-Pod affinity and anti-affinity with selectors, namespaces, topology keys, scheduler cost, and failure-domain consequences.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.config:
# kubernetes-basics cumulative orchestration portfolio
# Browser labs perform deterministic manifest and evidence review only.
# They never invoke kubectl, read kubeconfig, contact a cluster API, or execute host commands.
workspace: kubernetes
version-baseline:
  kubernetes: 1.36.2
  minikube: 1.38.1
  gateway-api: 1.5.1
  helm: 4.2.2
transfer-boundary: authorized-disposable-cluster
evidence:

steps.title: Predict K8s Taints Tolerations Conditions · Theory K8s Taints Tolerations Conditions
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory Pod is Pending with an untolerated taint, required anti-affinity, insufficient memory, and topology spread skew 2. Reconstruct filtering, scoring, requests, selectors, affinity, taints, preemption, and eviction without forcing nodeName. Competency probe: Trace NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule, and NoExecute taints, matching tolerations, toleration time, node conditions, and admission-added behavior.

content.title: Reasoning move
content.text: Name context, namespace, API identity, desired spec, observed status, controller authority, workload, network, storage, identity, policy, and failure-domain boundaries; predict reconciliation; review the smallest deterministic manifest change; then verify a failure, repair, rollback, and disposable-cluster transfer limit.

content.title: Worked case pattern
content.text: A worked example Pod is Pending with an untolerated taint, required anti-affinity, insufficient memory, and topology spread skew 2. Reconstruct filtering, scoring, requests, selectors, affinity, taints, preemption, and eviction without forcing nodeName. Competency probe: Trace NoSchedule, PreferNoSchedule, and NoExecute taints, matching tolerations, toleration time, node conditions, and admission-added behavior. A defensible solution applies K8s Taints Tolerations Conditions, preserves the governing invariant or value contract, and connects the observed result to placement and disruption decision record.

content.code:
# Evidence: k8s-worked-k8s-scheduling-placement-eviction-4
  - competency: k8s-worked-k8s-scheduling-placement-eviction-4
    artifact: placement-policy.yaml
    decision: worked k8s scheduling placement eviction 4
    environment: air-gapped release cluster using an internal registry mirror
    fault-injection: drop one metrics series during an autoscaling surge
    retained-proof: accessible incident timeline with causal regression evidence
    case-key: worked4
    change: |
      node-affinity: trusted-workload-class
      toleration: dedicated=intake:NoSchedule
      topologySpread: zones-maxSkew-1
      priorityClass: intake-critical
      preemption-policy: evidence-gated
      eviction: graceful-and-budgeted
    verify: kubectl get pod status-api -o wide && kubectl get events --field-selector involvedObject.name=status-api
    failure: Reject toleration-as-attraction, preferred-as-required, replica count as spread proof, or priority as permission for unbounded disruption.
    repair: Derive eligible nodes and domains, calculate skew, inspect unschedulable reasons, separate preemption from eviction, and preserve graceful recovery.
content.caption: Runnable Kubernetes object and transfer-evidence contract example; predict its result before running, then change one boundary input.

content.title: Non-worked case
content.text: A toleration attracts a Pod to the tainted node and guarantees placement there. This fails the K8s Taints Tolerations Conditions contract because it omits a precondition, invariant, ownership rule, or counterexample needed by this exact case.
